sections.instruction: Questions 1-5: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 6-10: Complete Harry’s notes using NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS.
sections.passage_or_context: Harry and Andrea are discussing what Harry should do with his possessions before going to England.

questions.content: What is Harry’s problem?
options: A. he does not want to sell his things
B. he needs to decide what to do with his possessions
C. he wants to take everything to England

questions.content: Which of the items below does Harry want to sell?
Where is Harry going to advertise his books for sale?
Andrea thinks it is unlikely students will buy the furniture because…
options: A. they’re all doing the same thing.

questions.content: Andrea thinks that a second hand shop…
options: B. may not take your goods
C. may only take free goods

questions.content:
Things to do:
• (6) {{BLANK}} furniture etc. in Trading Post
• (7) {{BLANK}} or sell kitchen things
• Get (8) {{BLANK}} first from second hand shop
• Give clothes to (9) {{BLANK}} shop
• (10) {{BLANK}} fridge and microwave to Andrea

sections.instruction: Questions 11-14: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 15-20: Complete the summary below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context: Information about the baths and the Great Bath site.

questions.content: What can visitors use free of charge?
Which of the following can visitors buy at the shop?
When did the Grand Opening of the baths occur?
The Romans built on the site
options: C. at the same time as the Celts

questions.content: The Great Bath is (15) {{BLANK}} deep. Around the bath area are alcoves where there were (16) {{BLANK}} and tables where bathers could relax. The water temperature of the Sacred Spring is (17) {{BLANK}}. The water is rich in (18) {{BLANK}}. In Roman times, the Sacred Spring was well known for its (19) {{BLANK}}. The Temple was constructed between (20) {{BLANK}}.

sections.instruction: Questions 21-23: Complete the sentences below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer. Questions 24-27: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 28-30: Choose THREE letters A-F.
sections.passage_or_context: A tutor and Fergus are discussing attending a jobs fair for engineering students.

questions.content: Fergus says that
options: A. there is one company he is particularly interested in
B. he has done some research already
C. he knows the boss at one of the companies

questions.content: The tutor thinks Fergus should
options: B. research the skills required for jobs before the event
C. find out what the starting salaries are

questions.content: Fergus plans
options: A. to wear a suit and tie
B. to wear smart but casual clothes
C. to buy an outfit for the event

questions.content: The tutor suggests that Fergus
options: A. should ask particular people certain questions
B. should avoid taking free gifts
C. should treat conversations like short interviews

questions.content: Why do the tutor and Fergus think it is useful to attend a jobs fair?
options: B. to find out what employers want from you
C. to give employers your contact details
D. to discover which are the key companies to work for
E. to practice your communication skills

sections.instruction: Question 31: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 32 and 33: Circle TWO letters A-D. Questions 34 and 35: Circle the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 36-40: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context: Rebecca gives a talk about challenges for new artists and suggestions for developing a portfolio.

questions.content: At the start of her talk Rebecca points out that new graduates can find it hard to
Which TWO of the following does Rebecca say worry new artists
options: B. moving to a new environment

questions.content: Rebecca decided to become an illustrator because it
options: A. afforded her greater objectivity as an artist
B. offered her greater freedom of expression
C. allowed her to get her work published

questions.content: When she had developed a portfolio of illustrations, Rebecca found publishers
options: A. more receptive to her work
B. equally cautious about her work
